items.text: It would have been too painful.
items.context: Clarke's reason for declining to watch or attend performances of Hugh Whitmore's 1987 play 'Breaking the Code,' which featured a character based on her and dramatized her relationship with Alan Turing
items.publicReaction: This statement was cited in multiple sources as evidence of how deeply the personal relationship with Turing affected her, even decades after the war and their broken engagement
items.source: MacTutor History of Mathematics Archive and biographical sources

items.context: Clarke's written chapter contribution to the edited volume 'Codebreakers: The Inside Story of Bletchley Park,' discussing her work on the naval Enigma cipher
items.publicReaction: Published contribution was well-received by historians and cryptography scholars as a first-hand account from one of the key personnel involved in breaking the naval Enigma
items.source: Codebreakers: The Inside Story of Bletchley Park, edited by F.H. Hinsley and Alan Stripp, Oxford University Press, pages 113-118

items.text: Appendix 30: The Polish, French and British contributions to the solution of the Enigma
items.context: Clarke co-authored an appendix with Sir Harry Hinsley correcting the historical record about the respective contributions of British, French, and Polish cryptanalysts to breaking the Enigma, addressing inaccuracies in earlier accounts
items.publicReaction: This scholarly contribution was important for historical accuracy and recognized Clarke's role in reassessing the Polish contribution to cryptanalysis during the war
items.source: British Intelligence in the Second World War, Volume 3, Part 2, revised edition
